blocks.text: DTC P0034: P2 relief valve control SOL.V. circuit voltage low

P2 relief valve control SOL.V. controls the timing of P2 relief by the PGM-FI ECU. the PGM-FI ECU improves the response of the boost pressure rise during re-acceleration immediately after deceleration and controls the reduction of boost pressure during deceleration. P2 relief valve control SOL.V. is normally CLOSE (negative pressure side). If the return signal remains OPEN for a predetermined period of time or longer while the PGM-FI ECU is outputting a CLOSE signal to the P2 relief valve control SOL.V., the PGM-FI ECU judges a failure and stores the DTC.
Detection frequency, detection sequence, detection time required, detection method type, OBD status
P2 relief valve If the return signal remains OPEN for 1 second or longer while the CLOSE signal is being output to the control SOL.V.
Turn ON/OFF the P2 relief valve control SOL.V. in the INSPECTION menu with HDS.

If the vehicle is determined to have a fault, a temporary DTC is stored in the PGM-FI ECU memory. If the same failure is detected at the next D/C (two consecutive detections), the PGM-FI warning light comes on and the DTC and freeze data are stored.

If the vehicle is diagnosed and determined to be normal after running consecutive 3D/C runs, the PGM-FI warning light will turn off. In addition, the PGM-FI warning light, temporary DTC, DTC and freeze data are cleared by using the clear command of the scan tool (including HDS) or by removing the 12V battery terminal.
